REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE, LLC'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, B&P 17200, NEGLIGENCE (MACDONALD), WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE (BROWN). THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE (BROWN) AND WRONGFUL FORECLOSURE (MACDONALD) AS THEY ARE NOT PROPER PLAINTIFFS UNDER THESE CAUSES OF ACTION. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF CC 2923.6 AS PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT NO MODIFICATION APPLICATION WAS PENDING AT THE TIME OF NOD, NOTS OR SALE. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT THE APPLICATION WAS APPROVED AND PLAINTIFF COMPLIED WITH THE TERMS OF THE APPROVAL. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT DUKE PARTNERS, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION PROVIDING FOR SUBSTANTIVE BASIS OF LIABILITY CHALLENGING THE FORECLOSURE. SEE DISTOR V. U.S. BANK (2009) 2009 WL 3429700; LOPEZ V. CHASE HOME FINANCIAL (2009) 2009 WL 981676; GLUE-FORD, INC. V. SLAUTTERBACK CORP. (2000) 82 CAL.APP.4TH 1018. CANCELLATION OF DEED AND QUIET TITLE ARE NOT PROPER MEANS TO "CHALLENGE THE FORECLOSURE." ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT SEEK DAMAGES AND PROSPECTIVE INJUNCTION, NO CAUSE OF ACTION SEEKS TO "UNDO THE FORECLOSURE." ADDITIONALLY, IF PLAINTIFFS CONTINUE TO ASSERT A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR QUIET TITLE IN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT PLAINTIFFS SHALL ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT MACDONALD'S STANDING IN LIGHT OF THE TRANSFER OF TITLE IN 2004 AND TO ALLEGE REPAYMENT OF DEBT. THE COURT DOES NOT ADDRESS DEFENDANT'S BFP ARGUMENT AT THIS TIME. THE COURT ALLOWS 20 DAYS TO AMEND COMPLAINT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. A BINDING, ENFORCEABLE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT WILL TOLL THE FIVE-YEAR STATUTE. (SEE, BROWN & BRYANT, INC. V. HARTFORD ACCIDENT & INDEMNITY CO.(1994) 24 CAL.APP.4TH 247, 254-56.) IMPLICIT IN NATIONSTAR'S REQUEST TO DISMISS IS THE REQUEST TO HAVE THE COURT FIND THAT PLAINTIFFS BREACHED THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IN ORDER TO FIND THAT TOLLING ENDED. THIS THE COURT WILL NOT DO ON A MOTION TO DISMISS. IF DEFENDANT BELIEVES THERE IS NO LONGER AN ENFORCEABLE SETTLEMENT, THEY CAN MOVE TO SET IT ASIDE AND RESET THE TRIAL DATE. JUDGE: CHARLES F. REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE, LLC, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. ON MAY 11, 2023 THIS COURT RULED THAT IT WOULD NOT BE ADDRESSING THE VALIDITY/ENFORCEABILITY OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES (REPORTED TO THE COURT ON AUGUST 17, 2021, WHICH RESULTED IN THE TRIAL BEING TAKEN OFF CALENDAR AND OCTOBER 2023 ORDER ENFORCING PARTS OF AUGUST 17, 2021 AGREEMENT) ON A MOTION TO DISMISS. THE COURT INSTRUCTED THE PARTIES TO FILE A SEPARATE MOTION TO ADDRESS THIS ISSUE. NO SUCH MOTION WAS FILED. INSTEAD, A REQUEST TO DETERMINE THE VALIDITY/INVALIDITY ENFORCEABILITY OF PARTIALLY COMPLIED WITH AND PARTIALLY ENFORCED SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT IS AGAIN BROUGHT UP WITHIN A MOTION TO DISMISS. THIS APPEARS TO BE A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER REGARDING THE PROCESS FOR ADDRESSING THE EXISTENCE/CONTINUED EXISTENCE/ENFORCEABILITY/EFFECT OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; REPORTER MARY FERREIRA, CSR 10553, MFERREIRA@CALREPOTER.NET, 510-928-6268. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar Mar-13-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Seth Cox Esq., The law offices of Seth Cox, 213-589-1042, via zoom for defendant, Duke Partners, LLC
Meghan Turner Esq., Wedgewood - Office of the General Counsel, 310-640-3070 via zoom for defendant, Duke Partners, LLC
Joel Spann Esq., Severson & Werson, 415-398-3344, via zoom for defendants, Nationstar Mortgage LLC and Wells Fargo Bank, NA.
Gibbs Brett Esq. TRN Law Associates, 415-823-4566, via zoom for intervenor

Mr. Spann contests the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE, LLC, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DISMISS is DENIED without prejudice. On May 11, 2023 this Court ruled that it would not be addressing the validity/enforceability of the settlement agreement between the parties (reported to the Court on August 17, 2021, which resulted in the trial being taken off calendar and October 2023 order enforcing parts of August 17, 2021 agreement) on a motion to dismiss. The Court instructed the parties to file a separate motion to address this issue. No such motion was filed. Instead, a request to determine the validity/invalidity enforceability of partially complied with and partially enforced settlement agreement is again brought up within a motion to dismiss. This appears to be a motion for reconsideration of the Court's prior order regarding the process for addressing the existence/continued existence/enforceability/effect of the settlement agreement.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reporter Mary Ferreira, CSR 10553, Mferreira@calrepoter.net, 510-928-6268. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss
Off Calendar May-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPERANCES:
Rhonda Nelson (415)398-3344 of Severson & Werson appearing via Zoom for defendants Nationstar Mortgage LLC et al.
Elaine Soong (310)640-3070 of Wedgewood Office of the General Counsel appearing via Zoom for defendant Duke Partners, LLC.
Brett Gibbs (415)823-4566 of TRN Law Associates appearing via Zoom- Intervenor
Reporter: JANE HONG; CSR# 11975; 909-831-2644; CSRJane@hotmail.com

Hearing held (See reporter's transcript for full details). Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT NATIONSTAR MORTGAGE, LLC, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss

is DENIED. A binding, enforceable settlement agreement will toll the five-year statute. (See, Brown & Bryant, Inc. v. Hartford Accident & Indemnity Co.(1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 247, 254-56.) Implicit in Nationstar's request to dismiss is the request to have the Court find that Plaintiffs breached the settlement agreement in order to find that tolling ended. This the Court will not do on a Motion to Dismiss. If Defendant believes there is no longer an enforceable settlement, they can move to set it aside and reset the trial date.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: L. Tauala ; Reporter: JANE HONG; CSR# 11975; 909-831-2644; CSRJane@hotmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jan-26-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Brett Gibbs; trn Law Associates; 415-823-4566; appearing for Judgment Creditor and Intervenor TRN Law Associates and Tiffany R. Norman.
Rhonda L. Nelson; Severson & Werson, APC; 415-398-3344; appearing for Defendants and Cross-Defendant Nationstar Mortgate LLC and defendant Wells Fargo Bank, National Association.
Meghan Turner; Wedgewood; 310-640-3070; appearing for defendant Duke Partners, LLC.

Counsel for Intervenor TRN Law Associates and Tiffany R. Norman contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

INTERVENOR'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is DENIED. The relief requested in this motion is not available under the MOU. The provisions that the moving party seeks to enforce (e.g. dismissal, un-consolidation, interpleader) are all expressly dependent on the execution of a settlement agreement (unlike other provisions that are enforceable upon the execution of the MOU). It appears from the response filed by Nationstar that this party is willing to interplead the amounts discussed in the MOU notwithstanding any evidence of submission of W-9 or execution of the settlement agreement, Nationstar may make a motion seeking such relief.

Counsel to prepare order. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendant Duke Partners, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings To Second Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DUKE PARTNERS, LLC'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Appearances: Elaine Yang of Wedgewood, Inc. (310.640.3070 x399) appearing via Court Call for defendant and moving party; Tiffany R. Norman of TRN Law Associates (415.823.4566) for plaintiff and responding party.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: 1.Motion is granted without leave to amend as to MacDonald who does not have standing to pursue cancellation of Deed and Quiet Title. MacDonald cites no authority for the proposition that a purported beneficial owner has standing to bring causes of action for cancelation of deed and quiet title seeking to be vested with beneficial title. 2. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to Brown to verify the complaint. Motion is otherwise denied. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that the foreclosure sale is void. Further, the Court indicates leave to amend shall be within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Elaine Yang (310-640-3070) of Wedgewood, Inc. for Defendant/Moving party DUKE PARTNERS, LLC. via Court Call
No Other Appearances

The Court announces its tentative ruling. Not ARGUED. Defendant seeks clarification on how many days they have to amend. The tentative ruling is adopted and modified as follows:

DEFENDANT DUKE PARTNERS, LLC's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a cause of action providing for substantive basis of liability challenging the foreclosure. See Distor v. U.S. Bank (2009) 2009 WL 3429700; Lopez v. Chase Home Financial (2009) 2009 WL 981676; Glue-Ford, Inc. v. Slautterback Corp. (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1018. Cancellation of Deed and Quiet Title are not proper means to "challenge the foreclosure." All other causes of action in the operative complaint seek damages and prospective injunction, no cause of action seeks to "undo the foreclosure." Additionally, if Plaintiffs continue to assert a cause of action for quiet title in the amended complaint Plaintiffs shall allege facts to support Macdonald's standing in light of the transfer of title in 2004 and to allege repayment of debt. The Court does not address Defendant's BFP argument at this time. The Court allows 20 days to amend complaint. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: A. James Robertson, II, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
